text: As the terminology Metaverse began to appear more frequently in media reports, the large technology companies began to express interest and the concepts associated with the Metaverse such as Web 3.0, blockchain, cryptocurrencies, and NFTs started to receive more attention from the public. Despite the growing number of media reports and the increasing discussion on the Metaverse, a scientific consensus regarding the meaning of this concept has not yet been reached in the scientific community. This paper addresses this need by applying a Systematic Literature Review (SLR) methodology to collect, analyze, and synthesize the definitions and their constitutive components related to the concept of the Metaverse. Following the analysis and synthesis of the various scientific definitions related to the Metaverse, two possible revised definitions of the term are formulated. The first is more holistic and simple, thus it describes the Metaverse as a three-dimensional online space where users and their respective avatars can express themselves in a virtual world separate from the real one. The second provides more technical detail and is suitable for more specialized scientific discussions. The paper will contribute to the fields of systems science, information systems, computer science, and business informatics with a common basis for referring to this term in future research studies. In addition, the paper will also address the open research challenges and possible social, economic, and technical impacts of the Metaverse, and highlight the prerequisites for achieving these proposed objectives.
